title: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park
tagline: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park is a protected area of India consisting of 21 small islands (islets) and adjacent coral reefs in the Gulf of Mannar & Adams Bridge in the In
overview.description: The Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park is a protected area of India consisting of 21 small islands (islets) and adjacent coral reefs in the Gulf of Mannar & Adams Bridge in the Indian Ocean. It lies 1 to 10 km away from the east coast of Tamil Nadu, India for 160 km between Thoothukudi (Tuticorin) and Dhanushkodi. It is the core area of the Gulf of Mannar Biosphere Reserve which includes a 10 km buffer zone around the park, including the populated coastal area. The park has a high diversity of plants and animals in its marine, intertidal and near shore habitats.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: India–Sri Lanka HVDC Interconnection
topPlaces.description: The India – Sri Lanka HVDC Grid Interconnection is a proposed project to link the national grids of India and Sri Lanka. The project involves the construction of a high-voltage direct current (HVDC) connection between Madurai in southern India, and Mannar in Northern Sri Lanka, through the Palk Strait.

topPlaces.name: Dhanushkodi
topPlaces.description: Dhanushkodi is an abandoned town at the south-eastern tip of Pamban Island of the state of Tamil Nadu in India. It is south-east of Pamban and is about 24 kilometres (15 mi) west of Talaimannar in Sri Lanka.

topPlaces.name: Adam's Bridge
topPlaces.description: Adam's Bridge, also known as Rama's Bridge or Rama Setu, is a chain of natural limestone shoals between Pamban Island, also known as Rameswaram Island, off the southeastern coast of Tamil Nadu, India, and Mannar Island, off the northwestern coast of Sri Lanka. Geological evidence suggests that the bridge was formerly a land connection between India and Sri Lanka.

topPlaces.name: Palk Strait Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Palk Strait Bridge and Tunnel is a proposed 23-kilometre (14 mi) road and rail bridge and tunnel over the Palk Strait between Dhanushkodi in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu and Talaimannar on Mannar Island off the northwestern coast of Sri Lanka. The project was first formally introduced in 2002 during peace talks in Sri Lanka, the project has since undergone multiple feasibility assessments, including recent bilateral agreements in 2023–24 to evaluate its technical and economic viability.

topPlaces.name: Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project
topPlaces.description: Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project (lit. 'Setu Ocean Shipping Canal Project') is a proposed project to create a shipping route in the shallow straits between India and Sri Lanka. This would provide a continuously navigable sea route around the Indian Peninsula.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — India–Sri Lanka HVDC Interconnection & around

items.note: The India – Sri Lanka HVDC Grid Interconnection is a proposed project to link the national grids of India and Sri Lanka. The project involves the construction of a high-voltage direct current (HVDC) connection between Madurai in southern India, and Mannar in Northern Sri Lanka, through the Palk Strait.

Dhanushkodi is an abandoned town at the south-eastern tip of Pamban Island of the state of Tamil Nadu in India. It is south-east of Pamban and is about 24 kilometres (15 mi) west of Talaimannar in Sri Lanka.

Adam's Bridge, also known as Rama's Bridge or Rama Setu, is a chain of natural limestone shoals between Pamban Island, also known as Rameswaram Island, off the southeastern coast of Tamil Nadu, India, and Mannar Island, off the northwestern coast of Sri Lanka. Geological evidence suggests that the bridge was formerly a land connection between India and Sri Lanka.

The Palk Strait Bridge and Tunnel is a proposed 23-kilometre (14 mi) road and rail bridge and tunnel over the Palk Strait between Dhanushkodi in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu and Talaimannar on Mannar Island off the northwestern coast of Sri Lanka. The project was first formally introduced in 2002 during peace talks in Sri Lanka, the project has since undergone multiple feasibility assessments, including recent bilateral agreements in 2023–24 to evaluate its technical and economic viability.

Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project (lit. 'Setu Ocean Shipping Canal Project') is a proposed project to create a shipping route in the shallow straits between India and Sri Lanka. This would provide a continuously navigable sea route around the Indian Peninsula.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (170 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (172 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are India–Sri Lanka HVDC Interconnection, Dhanushkodi, Adam's Bridge, Palk Strait Bridge, Sethusamudram Shipping Canal Project.
